subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Audit (from rulebook)
- **Supported count** — The base game is played with 2-4 players.
- **2 players** — Each player uses 2 kings, game uses 24 dominoes, line size is 4 dominoes, each player resolves D1+D2 twice per turn, game length is 6 turns.
- **3 players** — Each player uses 1 king, game uses 36 dominoes, line size is 3 dominoes, game length is 12 turns.
- **4 players** — Each player uses 1 king, game uses all 48 dominoes, line size is 4 dominoes, game length is 12 turns.
- **Variant (2 players)** — Mighty Duel uses all dominoes and a 7x7 kingdom.

#### Kingdom Pieces
- **Starting tiles** — 4 total (single-square tiles).
- **Castles** — 4 total (1 pink, 1 yellow, 1 green, 1 blue).

#### Dominoes
- **Domino tiles** — 48 total.
- **Tile sides** — Each domino has 1 landscape side and 1 numbered side.

#### Kings
- **Wooden kings** — 8 total.
- **Color split** — 2 pink, 2 yellow, 2 green, 2 blue.

#### Pre-Game Assembly
- **First play only** — Assemble the 4 small castles before starting.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Shared Draft Area
1. **Place box** — Put the game box in the play area as the draw reference point.
2. **Load draw pile** — Put selected dominoes in the box with numbered sides visible.
3. **Build current row** — Reveal a row next to the box in ascending number order, then flip to landscape side.
4. **Build next row** — Reveal a second row the same way once kings are assigned to the current row.

#### Player Kingdom Area
- **Personal layout** — Each player builds in front of themselves around their starting tile.
- **Hard boundary** — Every kingdom must fit inside a 5x5 grid.
- **Connection orientation** — Terrain matching uses orthogonal adjacency only (no diagonal matching).

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
--- 2 Players ---
- **Kings per player** — Take both kings of your chosen color (2 kings each).
- **Starting tile** — Take 1 starting tile and place it face up in front of you.
- **Castle** — Place matching-color castle on the starting tile.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Kings per player** — Take 1 king of your chosen color.
- **Starting tile** — Take 1 starting tile and place it face up in front of you.
- **Castle** — Place matching-color castle on the starting tile.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Kings per player** — Take 1 king of your chosen color.
- **Starting tile** — Take 1 starting tile and place it face up in front of you.
- **Castle** — Place matching-color castle on the starting tile.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Build the Domino Pool
--- 2 Players ---
- **Domino count in game** — Randomly select 24 dominoes to use.
- **Dominoes removed** — Set aside 24 random dominoes.
- **Kings in play** — 4 kings total.
- **Dominoes per row** — Draw 4 dominoes per row.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Domino count in game** — Randomly select 36 dominoes to use.
- **Dominoes removed** — Set aside 12 random dominoes.
- **Kings in play** — 3 kings total.
- **Dominoes per row** — Draw 3 dominoes per row.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Domino count in game** — Use all 48 dominoes.
- **Dominoes removed** — None.
- **Kings in play** — 4 kings total.
- **Dominoes per row** — Draw 4 dominoes per row.

#### First-Round Claim Procedure
1. **Create first row** — Sort drawn dominoes in ascending order (lowest nearest the box), then flip to landscape side.
2. **Random king draw** — One player mixes all kings in hand and reveals them one by one.
3. **Claim tiles** — Owner of each revealed king places it on any unclaimed domino in the row.
4. **One king per domino** — Last available king must take the final open domino.
5. **Create second row** — Draw a new row in ascending order and flip it.

--- 2 Players ---
- **Initial claims** — Each player claims 2 dominoes during this procedure (one per king).

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Core Round Flow
1. **Set order** — Resolve players by king position on the claimed row, from lowest number to highest.
2. **D1 (place)** — Active player places the domino with their king into their kingdom using connection and 5x5 rules.
3. **D2 (claim)** — Active player places that king onto one domino in the next row.
4. **Continue** — Repeat until all kings in the claimed row have taken D1 and D2.
5. **Refill row** — Reveal a new next row in ascending order and flip to landscape side.
6. **Start next turn** — Use the newly claimed row to determine the new order.

--- 2 Players ---
- **Activations per player each turn** — 2 (one per king).
- **Game length** — 6 turns total.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Activations per player each turn** — 1.
- **Game length** — 12 turns total.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Activations per player each turn** — 1.
- **Game length** — 12 turns total.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### D1: Place Your Claimed Domino
- **Primary legal anchor** — Connect to your starting tile (starter is wild; any terrain may touch it).
- **Alternative legal anchor** — Connect to an existing domino by matching at least 1 terrain edge orthogonally.
- **Grid limit** — Entire kingdom must remain inside a 5x5 area.
- **Illegal placement result** — If no legal placement exists, discard that domino.
- **Discard scoring** — Discarded dominoes score 0 points.

#### D2: Claim a New Domino
- **Selection rule** — Place your king on 1 unclaimed domino in the next row.
- **Exclusivity** — Each domino can hold only 1 king.
- **Initiative effect** — Lower-number claimed dominoes act earlier next turn.

#### Practical Lookup
- **Can I rotate dominoes?** — Yes, as long as placement remains legal.
- **Can I connect diagonally?** — No, terrain matching is horizontal/vertical only.
- **Can I exceed 5x5 temporarily?** — No, the placement itself must stay legal at time of placement.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Initiative Draft Tension
- **Early pick tradeoff** — Lower-number dominoes usually improve next-turn order.
- **Value tradeoff** — Higher-number dominoes may have stronger terrain/crown potential.
- **Decision pressure** — Every claim balances immediate scoring potential against future turn priority.

#### Spatial Compression
- **Hard cap puzzle** — All placements must resolve into a 5x5 kingdom.
- **Long-term risk** — One greedy placement can force later discards.
- **Best practice** — Preserve flexible edges and corners for awkward future domino shapes.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End Trigger
- **Final-turn trigger** — When the last dominoes are lined up, players take one last turn.
- **Last-turn action** — Resolve D1 placement only (no further D2 claiming).

#### How to Score
1. **Identify territories** — Group connected same-terrain squares (orthogonal connections only).
2. **Score each territory** — Territory score = number of squares × number of crowns in that territory.
3. **Apply zero-crown rule** — A territory with 0 crowns scores 0.
4. **Total score** — Add all territory scores for final points.

#### Winner and Tiebreakers
- **Primary win condition** — Highest final score wins.
- **Tiebreaker 1** — Largest single territory by square count wins.
- **Tiebreaker 2** — If still tied, most total crowns wins.
- **Tiebreaker 3** — If still tied, tied players share victory.

#### Optional Scoring Variants (Rulebook Additions)
- **Dynasty** — Play 3 rounds; highest cumulative score wins.
- **Middle Kingdom** — Score +10 if castle is in the center of your territory.
- **Harmony** — Score +5 if your kingdom is complete (no discarded dominoes).

--- 2 Players ---
- **Mighty Duel variant** — Use all dominoes and build a 7x7 kingdom.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### Early Turn Checklist
- **Keep shape open** — Leave at least 2-3 expansion directions around your starter tile.
- **Secure a crown lane** — Prioritize at least one crown in a terrain you can realistically extend.
- **Plan 5x5 early** — Visualize outer borders by turn 2 so you do not trap future placements.
- **Use starter tile as pivot** — Treat the wild starter as a bridge between terrain clusters.

--- 2 Players ---
- **Two-king planning** — Use one king to secure turn order and the other to secure point-rich dominoes.
- **Double-activation tempo** — Pre-plan both activations before your turn begins to avoid self-blocking.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Draft pressure** — Watch both opponents’ terrain needs before committing to high-number picks.
- **Row awareness** — With only 3 dominoes per row, denied picks happen faster; keep backup terrain plans.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Competition density** — Expect key crowns to disappear quickly; prioritize flexible dominoes over perfect dominoes.
- **Initiative leverage** — Early turn order swings are stronger with more competitors in each draft row.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Crownless expansion** — Large regions without crowns score 0, no matter how many tiles they contain.
- **Late boundary panic** — Ignoring 5x5 limits until final turns causes forced discards.
- **Order blindness** — Picking only for tile quality and ignoring next-turn initiative loses critical drafts.
- **Over-fragmentation** — Splitting one terrain into many tiny regions usually lowers multiplication value.
- **Edge lock** — Filling borders too tightly early leaves no legal placements for long or awkward dominoes.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Strategic Trade-Offs
- **Tempo vs power** — Take a lower-number tile for turn priority, or a higher-number tile for immediate scoring strength.
- **Merge vs hedge** — Grow one large scoring region, or maintain multiple medium regions to reduce draft risk.
- **Now-fit vs future-fit** — Make the best current placement, or preserve open geometry for stronger late tiles.

metadata.notes: Primary source: provided Kingdomino rulebook excerpt (EN/FR/DE sections). Core game fully covered; optional official variants noted in endgame.
